Tests if the square root of NaN returns NaN using the Math.sqrt function.

# Purpose
This code is a simple test script that verifies the behavior of the `Math.sqrt` function when it receives `NaN` (Not-a-Number) as an input. The script uses the `assert` function to check that the result of `Math.sqrt(NaN)` is also `NaN`, which is the expected behavior according to the IEEE 754 standard for floating-point arithmetic. This test ensures that the `Math.sqrt` function correctly handles edge cases involving non-numeric inputs. The script provides narrow functionality, focusing solely on this specific mathematical operation and its compliance with expected standards.
